title: Markets rally after US court deems Trump tariffs illegal
section_path: Markets rally after US court deems Trump tariffs illegal
section_path_text: Markets rally after US court deems Trump tariffs illegal
summary:
Main
Rising geopolitical tensions pushed energy markets higher. Industrial metals gained after Trump's reciprocal tariffs were deemed illegal. By Daniel Hynes
Image reference: Screenshot 2026-02-23 09.32.37.png | assets/2026-02-23_markets-rally-after-us-court-deems-trump-tariffs-illegal_img_screenshot2026-02-23093237_cc5d6ab9a1cf.png

Market Commentary
Copper led the base metals sector higher on Friday after the Supreme Court tariff decision.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Rising geopolitical tensions pushed energy markets higher. Industrial metals gained after Trump's reciprocal tariffs were deemed illegal.

children.title: Market Commentary
children.summary: Copper led the base metals sector higher on Friday after the Supreme Court tariff decision. The ruling strikes at the heart of Trump's agenda, an all-purpose tool that he has wielded against major trading partners.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: China's stockpiling of crude oil last year went a long way to keeping the market tight after OPEC started rolling back its production cuts. That stockpiling could continue in 2026 amid the increased risk of supply disruptions.
